prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The sequence begins with a black-and-white product shot of a rectangular package labeled “PAL Stainless Steel Injector Blades.” Large text above the package reads “5 BLADES 69¢,” and text below reads “FITS ALL INJECTOR RAZORS.” The package is centered against a plain studio-style background. A “Nostalgic X’er” watermark appears at the lower right.

It then cuts to an almost entirely black frame. No objects or people are visible, apart from the same “Nostalgic X’er” watermark in the lower right.

By the final sampled frame, the image has changed to a color shot of a boxed food product labeled “Carnation instant breakfast.” The box is positioned prominently in the center, with the brand name and product name visible. The background appears to include plants or foliage and vertical structural elements, but details are somewhat blurred. A small white dish or cup-like object appears near the lower right area of the product display. The “Nostalgic X’er” watermark remains in the lower right. The sampled frames show an abrupt visual shift from razor blades to a different packaged product, with no visible transition details in between.
